text: Foam rolling for 20 minutes immediately, 24, and 48 hours after intense exercise substantially reduces delayed-onset muscle soreness (DOMS) in the quadriceps, with moderate to large effect sizes (Cohen d 0.59–0.84).
Foam rolling after intense lower-body exercise improves sprint performance recovery, with small to large effect sizes (Cohen d 0.68–0.77) compared to no foam rolling.
Foam rolling after intense lower-body exercise improves power recovery (broad-jump distance), with small to large effect sizes (Cohen d 0.48–0.87).
Foam rolling after intense lower-body exercise improves dynamic strength-endurance recovery with a small to moderate effect size (Cohen d 0.54).
A foam rolling protocol of 20 minutes per session applied immediately, 24 hours, and 48 hours post-exercise is effective for reducing DOMS and restoring dynamic performance.
Foam rolling increases pressure-pain threshold in fatigued muscles, indicating reduced muscle tenderness in the days following intense exercise.
